proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, distribution consistent with patient age in years) is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Higher feature_001 (3-level ordinal 0/1/2, distribution suggestive of ECOG performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.
feature_014 (binary, prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months in the overall cohort.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
spearmanr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation between feature_015 and pfs_months is rho=+0.785. Decile means rise monotonically from 1.33 months (f015 30-52) to 7.45 months (f015 78-90).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: 5.27 (feature_001=0), 4.05 (=1), 2.91 (=2). Spearman rho=-0.360, p approx 0.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import ttest_ind
ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_014']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_014']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.70 months when feature_014=1 vs 5.06 months when feature_014=0 (diff=-1.35). Spearman rho=-0.303, p approx 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_019 (continuous 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8 — distribution consistent with serum albumin g/dL) predicts longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_025 (continuous 0-24.6, mean 3.8 — distribution consistent with WBC) predicts shorter pfs_months.
Patients with feature_023=1 (binary, prevalence 42%) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_006=1 (binary, prevalence 35%) have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_006=0.

feature_027 (binary, prevalence 20%) is positively associated with pfs_months — the only common binary feature with a univariate positive effect, suggesting it may represent a beneficial intervention/treatment.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import spearmanr
spearmanr(df['feature_019'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.103, p=4e-117. Higher feature_019 (albumin-like) -> longer PFS.

analyses.code: spearmanr(df['feature_025'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=-0.120, p=5.6e-160. Higher feature_025 -> shorter PFS.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.12 vs 4.45 (diff=-0.33), p approx 0; modest negative association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.11 vs 4.42 (diff=-0.31), p approx 0; modest negative association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_027']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_027']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.09 vs 4.12 months (diff=+0.97). Spearman rho=+0.122, p=5.4e-166.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regression of pfs_months on all 33 features, feature_027 retains a positive independent effect on pfs_months consistent with a beneficial treatment.
Multivariable regression confirms feature_001 (PS-like) and feature_014 retain large independent negative effects on pfs_months.
Most binary features (feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_031) have null adjusted effects, ruling them out as treatment-like.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2=0.859. feature_027 adjusted beta = +0.943 (SE 0.010), t=97.9, p approx 0. Largest positive adjusted effect among binary features.

analyses.code: Same OLS model.
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 adjusted beta = -1.171 per level (t=-205.7); feature_014 adjusted beta = -1.362 (t=-175.6). feature_015 adjusted beta = +0.176 per unit (t=+453.6). feature_019 +0.469 per unit; feature_025 -0.076 per unit; feature_023 -0.354; feature_006 -0.287; feature_022 -0.357.

analyses.code: Same OLS model — coefficients & p-values for low-effect binaries.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted p>0.1 for feature_010 (beta=+0.003), feature_012 (+0.000), feature_018 (+0.006), feature_020 (+0.026), feature_024 (-0.011), feature_031 (+0.010), feature_011 (+0.020), feature_016 (-0.008), feature_028 (-0.016), feature_002 (+0.039). None plausibly resembles a treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is the principal candidate treatment in this dataset; its main effect (+0.97 months unadjusted, +0.94 adjusted) represents an average effect that is likely heterogeneous across biomarker subgroups.

Treatment_027 assignment is independent of measured covariates (i.e., the data look like a randomized cohort), so estimated treatment effects can be interpreted causally without need for propensity adjustment.

analyses.code: Examined main effects of all binary features in OLS; feature_027 is the largest positive.
analyses.result_summary: Among all binaries, only feature_027 has a clearly positive PFS effect (+0.94 months adjusted). All other binaries are either near-null or negative. Treatment_027 is therefore the only plausible 'beneficial intervention' in the dataset.

analyses.code: For each feature f, ttest_ind / chi2_contingency between feature_027=1 and feature_027=0 distributions.
analyses.result_summary: All 33 covariates are balanced between feature_027 arms (every t-test/chi-square p>0.10; e.g., feature_015 mean 65.11 vs 64.96, p=0.16; feature_023 rate 0.420 vs 0.418, p=0.71; feature_005 mean 9.22 vs 9.19, p=0.86). Consistent with random assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is modified by feature_023 — in patients with feature_023=1 the treatment_027 effect is attenuated or null.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is modified by feature_006 — feature_006=1 attenuates or eliminates the treatment_027 benefit.
The treatment effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is modified by the rare biomarker feature_022 — feature_022=1 attenuates or eliminates the benefit.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_023 + feature_027:feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-1.629, p=7.7e-226. The treatment effect of feature_027 is reduced by approximately 1.63 months when feature_023=1 (essentially abolished).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_006 + feature_027:feature_006.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-1.454, p=4.1e-168. feature_006=1 nearly abolishes the treatment effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + feature_022 + feature_027:feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-1.183, p=6.1e-23. Although feature_022 is rare (4.5%), its presence robustly attenuates the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_027 PFS benefit is concentrated in the subgroup where feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0; in any other stratum (feature_023=1 or feature_006=1) the treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: for v23,v06 in product([0,1],[0,1]): ttest_ind(treated, control) within stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Stratum (feature_023=0, feature_006=0): treated PFS 6.64 vs control 4.12 (diff=+2.52, N=18,853, p approx 0). Stratum (0,1): diff=+0.07, p=0.16. Stratum (1,0): diff=+0.06, p=0.17. Stratum (1,1): diff=-0.04, p=0.52. Effect is restricted to (feature_023=0 & feature_006=0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type subgroup (feature_023=0 & feature_006=0), the treatment_027 benefit is further restricted to patients with feature_022=0; if feature_022=1 the treatment_027 effect is null even in the wild-type subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest of treatment_027 effect by feature_022 within (f023=0 & f006=0).
analyses.result_summary: Within (f023=0, f006=0, f022=0): N=17,372; treated PFS 6.88 vs control 4.12 (diff=+2.76, p approx 0). Within (f023=0, f006=0, f022=1): N=1,481; treated PFS 3.94 vs control 4.14 (diff=-0.20, p=0.12). feature_022=1 abolishes treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type subgroup (feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0), the treatment_027 effect is also modified by the continuous feature_005 — higher feature_005 attenuates the benefit.
feature_005 main effect on PFS is essentially null (i.e., it is purely PREDICTIVE of treatment effect, not prognostic for baseline outcome).

analyses.code: OLS within wild-type: pfs_months ~ feature_027 + log1p(feature_005) + feature_027:log1p(feature_005).
analyses.result_summary: Interaction (f027 x log1p(f005)) beta=-2.180, p approx 0 (t=-48.9). Main treatment_027 beta in low-f005 reference is +6.629. Strong predictive role for feature_005.

analyses.code: Within untreated (feature_027=0) wild-type subgroup, mean PFS by feature_005 thresholds.
analyses.result_summary: In untreated wild-type patients, mean PFS is 4.09 (f005<=5) vs 4.15 (f005>5), 4.10 (<=10) vs 4.16 (>10), 4.11 (<=20) vs 4.18 (>20). Differences are <0.07 months — feature_005 is essentially non-prognostic in absence of treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type subgroup (f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0), there is a clean threshold near feature_005=5 such that the treatment_027 benefit is large for feature_005 <= 5 and essentially zero for feature_005 > 5.

analyses.code: for thr in [2,2.5,3,...,7]: compute treatment effect in low and high subgroups.
analyses.result_summary: Wild-type, f005<=2: diff=+5.04; <=3: +5.03; <=4: +5.04; <=4.47 (median): +5.03; <=5: +5.01; <=5.5: +4.78; <=6: +4.54. High side at thr=5: diff=-0.002 (null). Threshold ~5 produces the cleanest separation: +5.0 below, ~0 above.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full responder subgroup is feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5; within this subgroup, treatment_027 yields a +5 month PFS benefit, while outside it the effect is null.

analyses.code: biom_neg = (f023==0)&(f006==0)&(f022==0)&(f005<=5); compare treatment effects inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (N=9,338, 18.7% of cohort): treated PFS 9.10 vs control 4.09 (diff=+5.01, p approx 0). Complement (N=40,662): diff=+0.024, p=0.36 (null).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A nonparametric, tree-based estimator of treatment_027 individual effect on pfs_months will identify the same biomarker-defined responder subgroup.

analyses.code: Two-model gradient boosting (sklearn): mu1(X) trained on treated, mu0(X) trained on control; tau_hat = mu1(X)-mu0(X). Compare feature distributions in tau_hat>1 vs tau_hat<=1.
analyses.result_summary: Estimated tau distribution bimodal: ~80% of patients have tau_hat near 0 and ~18% have tau_hat near +5. In tau_hat>1 group: feature_023 rate 0.000, feature_006 rate 0.006, feature_022 rate 0.008, feature_005 mean 2.30. In tau_hat<=1 group: feature_023 rate 0.516, feature_006 rate 0.434, feature_022 rate 0.054, feature_005 mean 10.81. Other 30 features are balanced. Confirms manual subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features that have near-null main effects (feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_031) do NOT have a hidden positive treatment-like effect in any 1- or 2-feature subgroup; they are not treatments.

analyses.code: For each B in {f010, f012, f018, f020, f024, f031}, scan all 1-modifier and 2-modifier subgroups; report the largest |effect| with p<0.005 of B on PFS.
analyses.result_summary: No subgroup search returned any |diff|>0.5 with p<0.005 for these binaries — across both single-modifier and dual-modifier (within wild-type) searches, the maximum signed effect found was 0.0 (no significant subgroups). They are not treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 is purely predictive (modifies treatment effect) and not prognostic — among untreated (feature_027=0) patients with wild-type biomarkers, feature_005 has no association with PFS.

analyses.code: Among feature_027=0 & f023=0 & f006=0 & f022=0 patients, mean PFS by feature_005<=5 vs >5.
analyses.result_summary: In untreated wild-type: PFS 4.09 (f005<=5) vs 4.15 (f005>5), diff -0.06 months. Effectively no main effect. The huge effect we see in treated patients reflects predictive (treatment-modifying) action only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (performance-status-like, 0/1/2) does NOT modify the treatment_027 effect — within the responder subgroup the +5 month treatment effect is approximately uniform across feature_001 levels.

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, ttest of treatment_027 effect by feature_001 stratum.
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0: diff=+2.67 (within (f23=0,f06=0,f22=0)); =1: +2.77; =2: +2.74. In the further-restricted f005<=5 subgroup the effect is +5.0 across all PS levels. feature_001 is purely prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 does NOT modify the treatment_027 effect — uniform across feature_014 strata.
feature_015 (age-like) does NOT modify the treatment_027 effect — uniform across age tertiles.

analyses.code: Within (f023=0,f006=0,f022=0,f005<=5), ttest of f027 effect by feature_014.
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=0: diff=+5.03 (N=4097); feature_014=1: diff=+5.01 (N=5241). No modification.

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, ttest of f027 effect by feature_015 tertile.
analyses.result_summary: Age tertiles: lower (30-60.5) +4.95; middle (60.6-69.4) +5.07; upper (69.5-90) +5.07. Effect is essentially uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (albumin-like) does NOT modify treatment_027 — although it is prognostic for baseline PFS, it does not modify the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, ttest of f027 effect by feature_019 tertile.
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 tertile 0 (1.90-3.60): treatment diff=+4.96; tertile 1 (3.70-4.00): +5.03; tertile 2 (4.10-5.50): +5.12. Differences are <0.2 — not a meaningful modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (rare binary, prevalence 3%) was identified earlier as having a positive interaction with feature_027 in the full cohort, but within the wild-type/low-f005 subgroup it does NOT enhance the treatment effect — the apparent enhancement reflected confounding with the wild-type biomarker pattern.

analyses.code: Within (f023=0,f006=0,f022=0), ttest of f027 effect stratified by feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=0: treatment diff=+2.75 (N=16,443); feature_011=1: diff=+2.91 (N=929). Difference of differences ~0.16 — not meaningfully elevated. Within the further-restricted responder subgroup, feature_011 has no impact on treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (performance status), feature_014, feature_015, feature_019, feature_025 are independent prognostic factors for pfs_months when adjusted simultaneously, but not predictive of treatment_027 effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_027 + biom_neg + feature_027:biom_neg + feature_015 + feature_001 + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025.
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for prognostic covariates, treatment_027 main effect ~0 (beta=-0.004), biom_neg main effect ~0, but treatment_027:biom_neg interaction = +4.98 (t=905.6). Prognostic features retain stable estimates: feature_015 +0.176/unit, feature_001 -1.166/level, feature_014 -1.368, feature_019 +0.485/unit, feature_025 -0.078/unit. Adjustment does not change the responder-subgroup treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the median PFS approximately doubles with feature_027 — non-parametric test (Mann-Whitney) confirms the parametric finding.

analyses.code: mannwhitneyu(treated_responder['pfs_months'], control_responder['pfs_months'], alternative='greater').
analyses.result_summary: In responder subgroup: treated median PFS 9.09 vs control median 4.09. Mann-Whitney one-sided p approx 0. Distribution clearly shifted upward in treated arm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The threshold of feature_005=5 is the optimal split — alternative thresholds (4, 6, 7, 10) reduce subgroup specificity by either contaminating the high-effect group or shrinking the low-effect group.

analyses.code: for thr in [3,4,5,6,7,10]: compare diff_low and diff_high in wild-type (f23=0,f06=0,f22=0) cohort.
analyses.result_summary: Thresholds: f005<=3 -> diff_low +5.03 / diff_high +1.32; <=4 -> +5.04 / +0.69; <=5 -> +5.01 / -0.00; <=6 -> +4.54 / -0.02; <=7 -> +4.13 / +0.02. Threshold 5 maximizes separation (high side null).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Verify final subgroup definition with a 16-cell stratified table: treatment_027 effect equals +5 months only in the cell (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5); all other 11 testable cells have null effects.

analyses.code: for v23,v06,v22,v5 in product([0,1],[0,1],[0,1],[0,1]): test treatment effect.
analyses.result_summary: Of 16 cells, 12 had sufficient size. Only cell (0,0,0,1) [f005<=5] showed effect +5.01 (p approx 0). All other cells: |diff|<=0.25, p>0.10. The effect is fully concentrated in this single cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_005 has a roughly piecewise-linear effect on the treatment-027 benefit: full benefit (~+5 months) when feature_005 <= ~5, gradually nulled as feature_005 grows beyond 5.

analyses.code: Within wild-type, treatment effect by feature_005 deciles.
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 deciles in wild-type: D0-D4 (f005 0.04-4.47): treatment diff +4.93 to +5.11. D5 (4.47-6.02): +1.75. D6+ (>6.02): all near 0 (-0.08 to +0.04). The transition is sharp around f005=5-6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no meaningful enhancement of the +5 month treatment effect from any additional covariate (binary or continuous) beyond the four already identified — the responder subgroup is precisely the conjunction (feature_023=0 & feature_006=0 & feature_022=0 & feature_005<=5).

analyses.code: Within (f023=0,f006=0,f022=0,f005<=5): for each remaining feature, test if it stratifies the treatment effect further (would reveal a tighter sub-subgroup).
analyses.result_summary: Across remaining features tested as stratifiers within the responder subgroup (feature_001, feature_014, feature_015 tertile, feature_019 tertile, feature_011), no stratum produced a treatment difference materially larger than +5.0 (all stratum-specific effects were 4.95-5.12). The subgroup definition is complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023, feature_006, feature_022 are not only treatment-effect modifiers — they have small but real negative main effects on PFS (i.e., they are mildly prognostic), but their dominant role is to abolish treatment_027 efficacy.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features (no interactions). Compare adjusted main-effect coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: feature_023 -0.354 (t=-43.9), feature_006 -0.287 (t=-35.5), feature_022 -0.357 (t=-18.9). All p approx 0. These small main effects are dwarfed by their treatment-effect-abolishing role (interaction magnitudes -1.6, -1.5, -1.2 respectively).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: feature_027 is a beneficial intervention that prolongs pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months exclusively in patients meeting the conjunction (feature_023=0 AND feature_006=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005 <= 5). In every patient in whom any of these four predicates is unfavorable (feature_023=1 OR feature_006=1 OR feature_022=1 OR feature_005>5), the treatment_027 effect on pfs_months is zero (within statistical noise). Direction: treatment_027=1 increases pfs_months in the subgroup. The four-predicate conjunction is necessary; removing any one predicate degrades subgroup specificity. The pattern is consistent with a targeted therapy where feature_023, feature_006, and feature_022 act like resistance mutations and feature_005 is a biomarker of disease state where disease beyond a threshold (>5) renders the therapy ineffective.

analyses.code: Final ATE: ttest_ind in subgroup vs complement; OLS with 4-way subgroup interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup (n=9,338; 18.7% of cohort): mean PFS treated 9.10 vs control 4.09; Welch p approx 0; Mann-Whitney p approx 0; estimated ATE +5.014 months. Complement subgroup (n=40,662; 81.3%): mean PFS treated 4.15 vs control 4.12; diff +0.024; p=0.36 (null). OLS with the conjunction subgroup interaction: interaction coefficient +4.98 (p approx 0) after adjusting for feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025. The four-predicate subgroup defines the entire treatment effect.
